question_text: A wholesaler offers 15% discount if you pay Rs. 30,000 cash in advance but your wallet is tight. What do you do?
options.B.text: Pay and hope sales recover fast.
options.C.text: Borrow from a trusted peer merchant to secure the discount.
options.D.text: Pay higher price on delivery — no advance risk.

question_text: A neighbor asks for Rs. 5,000 goods on informal Udharo credit with no ledger. How do you respond?
options.A.text: Insist on writing down terms digitally before handing goods.
options.B.text: Provide it — close neighbors deserve trust.
options.C.text: Give Rs. 2,000 max as a test, set a strict repayment deadline.
options.D.text: Report them to the bazaar committee.

question_text: Your NEA electricity bill has an error. It will need an office visit to fix. What do you do?
options.A.text: Pay via eSewa immediately to stay on-time. Dispute later.
options.B.text: Delay until I visit the office next week.
options.D.text: Pay and skip the dispute to avoid hassle.

question_text: You want to buy a new commercial refrigerator. How do you fund it?
options.A.text: Save incrementally in my wallet for 6 months then buy.
options.B.text: Take a high-interest local loan and buy today.
options.C.text: Apply for a digital micro-credit loan matched to my revenue.
options.D.text: Buy second-hand with available cash flow now.

question_text: Sales drop 40% for 3 weeks due to a local festival. How do you manage cash flow?
options.A.text: I have a buffer savings account specifically for slow periods.
options.B.text: Reduce stock orders immediately and stretch payment terms.
options.C.text: Borrow from family and repay when sales recover.
options.D.text: Keep ordering the same stock and hope for quick recovery.

question_text: A digital payment shows an error but the money landed in your account. What do you do?
options.A.text: Report it to the payment provider and customer immediately.
options.B.text: Note it and inform both parties when convenient.
options.C.text: Wait and see if the customer or provider notices.
options.D.text: Keep it — their system error, not my problem.

question_text: How consistently do you track your daily income and expenses?
options.A.text: Daily — I have a notebook or digital app for it.
options.B.text: Weekly — I tally at week's end.

question_text: A trusted friend asks you to guarantee their Rs. 200,000 business loan. What do you do?
options.A.text: Agree only after seeing their full financials and plan.
options.B.text: Agree to a partial guarantee to limit my exposure.
options.C.text: Decline politely to protect my own credit standing.

question_text: An emergency (illness, flood) hits. How does it affect your business repayments?
options.A.text: I have emergency savings set aside separately.
options.B.text: I'd proactively contact the lender to reschedule.
options.C.text: Family first — I'd repay late and apologize.
options.D.text: I haven't thought about this scenario.

question_text: When do you decide to expand your business (new products, larger space)?
options.A.text: Only when I have 6+ months of consistent profit growth.
options.B.text: When a clear opportunity with manageable risk appears.
options.D.text: As soon as I feel confident about it.
